name: Sounding the Paper Garden
description:
Join us for a special listening session that centers around the original two-hour musical score created for our latest exhibition, Paper Gardens: Art, Botany and Empire 

The musical score was created on commission by Rohan Kale and Udai Sharma. A blend of field recordings from forests, gardens, and natural habitats across the country, were arranged with digitally produced instrumentation. 

Botanical illustrations— made on stark white backgrounds — tend to isolate a plant from its environment and ecological relationships. Through careful layering and spatial design, this score evokes the textures, rhythms, and seasonal transformations that suffuse the ecosystems that plants inhabit. It brings together recordings from forests, gardens, and natural habitats across the country, alongside digitally produced instrumentation. 

The session will begin with a behind-the-scenes walkthrough of the artists’ recording and composition process: how they sourced specific botanical sounds, their experiments with timbre and tone, and the challenges of translating ecological context into sonic experience. This will be followed by a listening session within the gallery space, surrounded by the artworks, offering participants a chance to experience how sound interacts with visual art and ambient space to shape memory, emotion, and perception.

Whether you’re a musician, listener, artist, botanist, or curious visitor, we welcome you to sit with us—and the plants—as the air comes alive with the sound of life unfolding in nature.

name: Curator’s POV
description:
How has the most meme’able, memorable and mischievous animal known to man been represented across art history in the subcontinent?

Join curator Khushi Bansal as she explores the many lives of the Indian cat—appearing in paintings, sculptures, matchbox covers, artist sketchbooks, and beyond.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Haru’s Paper Garden: A Children's Reading & Activity
description:
Image credit: Smit Zaveri and Barkha Lohia
Through imagined letters and storytelling, Haru’s Paper Garden sheds light on the life of Vishnuprasad, an artist whose contributions remain largely unrecorded.

In this reading and activity session, author Smit Zaveri invites young audiences to think about art, memory and the stories that history forgets.

name: Miniature to Mischief
description:
Image Credits: Sanchita Jain
Join us for a lively conversation about cats, their histories, myths, and personalities. Exploring two books on Indian cats, B. N. Goswamy's The Indian Cat and Olliewanders, you can swap stories, challenge common misconceptions, and celebrate the surprising complexity of our feline companions.

Expect good conversation, plenty of colour, and a "mosaic of many cats" who may look suspiciously like their humans, especially for anyone who suspects they might, spiritually, be a cat.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Paper Gardens ನೋಡಕ್ಕೆ ಬನ್ನಿ
description:
Join us for a guided walk of the exhibition Paper Gardens: Art, Botany, and Empire in Kannada by Rajesh K from the MAP Team.

Between the 17th and 20th centuries, the Indian subcontinent was the site of extensive botanical surveys under colonial rule. Plant collection and classification were closely tied to imperial economic, medical, and political interests, and botanical illustration played a central role in this project. Bringing together a new and historically significant collection of botanical art from this period, the exhibition draws on recent scholarship to recover the often uncredited identities and histories of the Indian artists who made them.

‘Paper Gardens’ marks the continuation of a sustained, years-long collaboration between MAP and Impart — an online platform fostering greater public engagement with the art and cultural histories of South Asia.

name: Small Worlds, Many Cats
description:
Learn traditional miniature techniques with artist Mahaveer, using the cat as your muse while exploring line, detail, and colour.

This hands-on workshop brings together storytelling, craftsmanship, and the feline’s enduring charm in Indian art.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.

name: Write from Art, Write from Story
description:
Explore the layered presence of the cat as muse, metaphor, and companion through a creative writing workshop led by writer and editor Indira Chandrasekhar.

This event is part of the closing weekend celebrations for The Many Lives of the Cat.
